2606.13218.title: When Similar Means Different: Evaluating LLMs on Arabic--Hebrew Cognates
2606.13218.paper_summary: Arabic and Hebrew, as closely related Semitic languages, share a substantial lexicon of true cognates, misleading false friends, and modern loanwords. This overlap poses a challenge for cross-lingual semantic understanding in large language models (LLMs). To evaluate this capability, we introduce SemCog Bench, a curated benchmark of 1,858 Arabic--Hebrew word pairs with sentence-level annotations for cognate identification and semantic disambiguation. We evaluate open-source and commercial LLMs across multiple input representations (raw, diacritized, Romanized, and phonetic) and reveal a critical gap in cross-lingual reasoning. While models achieve high accuracy on true cognates, performance drops sharply on false friends and loanwords, reflecting a strong reliance on surface-form similarity. Furthermore, sentence-level context yields only modest improvements, suggesting that contextual cues alone are insufficient to overcome misleading form-based signals. These findings reveal a fundamental limitation of current LLMs in resolving cross-lingual form--meaning conflicts and establish SemCog Bench as a rigorous benchmark for multilingual semantic reasoning. Our code and data are publicly available.
2606.13218.paper_summary_zh: 阿拉伯語和希伯來語作為密切相關的閃米特語言，擁有大量的真同源詞、誤導性的假朋友和現代借詞。這種重疊對大型語言模型（LLMs）的跨語言語義理解構成挑戰。為了評估這一能力，我們推出了SemCog Bench，一個精心策劃的基準，包含1,858對阿拉伯語和希伯來語的單詞對，並附有句子級別的註釋以便識別同源詞和語義消歧。 我們評估了多個開源和商業LLMs在多種輸入表示（原始、帶有變音符號的、羅馬化的和語音的）下的表現，並揭示了跨語言推理中的一個關鍵差距。雖然模型在真同源詞上達到高準確率，但在假朋友和借詞上的表現急劇下降，反映出對表面形式相似性的強烈依賴。此外，句子級別的上下文僅帶來適度的改善，這表明僅依賴上下文線索不足以克服誤導性的基於形式的信號。這些發現揭示了當前LLMs在解決跨語言形式與意義衝突方面的基本局限性，並確立了SemCog Bench作為多語言語義推理的嚴謹基準。我們的代碼和數據是公開可用的。
2606.13218.author: Junhong Liang et.al.
2606.13218.authors: Junhong Liang, Noor Abo Mokh, Bashar Alhafni
